vertical_split_selection.sort_order: descending by non_pk_column_count, then table name

base_predicate_sqls: ((("Extra_Runs" IS NOT NULL AND "Extra_Runs" > 1 AND "Extra_Runs" <= 1) OR ("Extra_Runs" IS NOT NULL AND "Extra_Runs" > 1 AND "Extra_Runs" <= 1)) OR ("Extra_Runs" > 1 OR "Extra_Runs" IS NULL))
fragment_roles: rows where 1 < Extra_Runs <= 1 + rows where 1 < Extra_Runs <= 1 + rows where Extra_Runs > 1 or is NULL
Extra_Runs||vfrag=extra.fragment_role_B: rows where 1 < Extra_Runs <= 1 + rows where 1 < Extra_Runs <= 1 + rows where Extra_Runs > 1 or is NULL

fragment_roles: rows where Win_Margin > 21 or is NULL
rows where Bowler > 293 or is NULL

base_predicate_sqls: (("Runs_Scored" IS NOT NULL AND "Runs_Scored" > 1 AND "Runs_Scored" <= 1) OR ("Runs_Scored" > 1 OR "Runs_Scored" IS NULL))
fragment_roles: rows where 1 < Runs_Scored <= 1 + rows where Runs_Scored > 1 or is NULL

justification.cross_local_query_count_interpretation: Distinct cross-local workload queries where this fragment pair (different original tables) co-occurs; not an estimate of single-local queries after relocation.

relocation_engine_debug.pair_weight_meaning: Weights count co-occurrences of two fragments (from different original tables) inside workload queries that already span 2+ locals. They rank relocation candidates; they do not equal the number of queries that will become single-local after a move.
